name: HMS Untamed (P 58)
events: The history of HMS Untamed / HMS Vitality as compiled on this page is extracted from HMS Untamed / HMS Vitality patrol reports and logbooks.
This page was last updated in February 2018.
21 Mar 1943 HMS Untamed (Lt. G.M. Noll, RN) conducted torpedo discharge trials off Arrochar. (1)
22 Mar 1943 HMS Untamed (Lt. G.M. Noll, RN) conducted torpedo discharge trials off Arrochar. (1)
26 Mar 1943 During the night of 26 / 27 March 1943, HMS Untamed (Lt. G.M. Noll, RN), conducted night exercises in the Clyde area. (1)
27 Mar 1943 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) participated in A/S exercises off Tobermory. (2)
29 Mar 1943 HMS Untamed (Lt. G.M. Noll, RN) departed the Tyne for Blyth where she arrived later the same day. (3)
29 Mar 1943 During the night of 29 / 30 March 1943, HMS Untamed (Lt. G.M. Noll, RN), conducted night exercises in the Clyde area. (1)
2 Apr 1943 HMS Untamed (Lt. G.M. Noll, RN) conducted exercises off Blyth. (1)
4 Apr 1943 HMS Untamed (Lt. G.M. Noll, RN) conducted exercises off Blyth. (1)
8 Apr 1943 HMS Untamed (Lt. G.M. Noll, RN) conducted exercises off Blyth. (1)
10 Apr 1943 HMS Untamed (Lt. G.M. Noll, RN) departed Blyth for Holy Loch. (1)
13 Apr 1943 HMS Untamed (Lt. G.M. Noll, RN) arrived at Holy Loch. (1)
14 Apr 1943 HMS Untamed (Lt. G.M. Noll, RN) conducted her acceptance trials and was then officially commissioned into the Royal Navy. She then started a training period.
16 Apr 1943 HMS Untamed (Lt. G.M. Noll, RN) conducted exercises in the Clyde area. (1)
17 Apr 1943 HMS Untamed (Lt. G.M. Noll, RN) conducted gunnery exercises in the Clyde area. (1)
18 Apr 1943 HMS Untamed (Lt. G.M. Noll, RN) conducted exercises in the Clyde area. (1)
20 Apr 1943 HMS Untamed (Lt. G.M. Noll, RN) shifted from Holy Loch to Arrochar. (1)
23 Apr 1943 HMS Untamed (Lt. G.M. Noll, RN) shifted from Arrochar to Holy Loch. (1)
25 Apr 1943 HMS Untamed (Lt. G.M. Noll, RN) conducted trials in the Clyde area. (1)
26 Apr 1943 HMS Untamed (Lt. G.M. Noll, RN) departed Holy Loch for Londonderry (Lough Foyle). (1)
27 Apr 1943 HMS Untamed (Lt. G.M. Noll, RN) arrived at Londonderry (Lough Foyle) to participate in A/S exercises. (1)
27 Apr 1943 HMS Untamed (Lt. G.M. Noll, RN) conducted exercises in the Clyde area. (1)
28 Apr 1943 HMS Untamed (Lt. G.M. Noll, RN) conducted exercises in the Clyde area. (1)
29 Apr 1943 HMS Untamed (Lt. G.M. Noll, RN) departed Londonderry (Lough Foyle) for Campbeltown to participate in A/S exercises from that port. (1)
29 Apr 1943 HMS Untamed (Lt. G.M. Noll, RN) conducted exercises off Lough Foyle with USCGC Duane (Capt. H.G. Bradbury, USCG) and HMS Keppel (Cdr. M.J. Evans, OBE, RN). (1)
30 Apr 1943 HMS Untamed (Lt. G.M. Noll, RN) returned to Holy Loch. Later she spent a few days at Larne before returning to Holy Loch on 24 May 1943. (1)
30 Apr 1943 HMS Untamed (Lt. G.M. Noll, RN) conducted exercises in the Clyde area followed by RD/F trials. (1)

30 May 1943 Upon completion of an A/S exercise off Campbeltown with HMS Shemara (Cdr.(Retd.) H. Buckle, RN), HMS Untamed (Lt. G.M. Noll, RN) failed to surface. She had failed to respond to charges fired by HMS Shemara since 1501 hours.

At 1600 hours HMS Shemara made a signal reporting the situation. The exercise was due to end at 1700 hours but Untamed failed to surface. Shemare meanwhile had heard the sounds of her engines being run and tanks being blown.
At 1745 hours HMS Shemara reported she had obtained a contact with her Asdic on Untamed laying on the bottom.
Salvage / diving vessel HMS Tedworth (Lt.Cdr.(Retd.) G.H. Warren, RN) was ordered to the scene but when she arrived divers could not be sent down due to the tide.
The next day diving operations were not possible due to the bad weather.
On 1 June it was decided that there was no hope for any survivors. Divers were able to go down this day and found Untamed upright on the seabed.

On 5 June it was reported that divers had found both escape hatches shut but that the after one was not secured. When they opened the outer hatch the body of the Chief Engine-room Artificer was found. His body was landed at Campbeltown.
In late June HMS Untamed was salvaged.
During 16/17 July 1943 HMS Untamed was towed from Campbeltown to Barrow for reconditioning.

The cause of the accident was that the forward part of the submarine had been flooded through a sluice valve of the ships logs being installed incorrectly. Untamed was therefore not able to surface. An attempt was made to escape from the engine room but this attempt failed due to yet another incorrectly installed valve. (4)
19 Jul 1944 HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) departed Barrow for Holy Loch. She was escorted by HMS Cutty Sark. (5)
20 Jul 1944 HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) arrived at Holy Loch to begin a period of trials and training. (5)
23 Jul 1944 HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) conducted exercises in the Clyde area. (5)
24 Jul 1944 HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) conducted exercises in the Clyde area. (5)
25 Jul 1944 HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) conducted exercises in the Clyde area. (5)
27 Jul 1944 HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) conducted noise trials in Loch Goil. (5)
28 Jul 1944 HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) conducted trials in the Clyde area. (5)
29 Jul 1944 HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) conducted exercises in the Clyde area with HMS Bridgewater (Cdr.(Retd.) R.H. Mack, RN). (5)
30 Jul 1944 During the night of 29 / 30 July 1944, HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR), conducted night exercises in the Clyde area with HMS Bridgewater (Cdr.(Retd.) R.H. Mack, RN). (5)
1 Aug 1944 HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) shifted from Holy Loch to Arrochar. (6)
3 Aug 1944 HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) conducted torpedo discharge trials off Arrochar. (6)
4 Aug 1944 HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) conducted torpedo discharge trials off Arrochar. Upon completion of these trials she returned to Holy Loch. (6)
6 Aug 1944 HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) conducted exercises in the Clyde area with HMS Shikari (Lt. E.H.U. Cautley, RNVR). (6)

6 Aug 1944 HMS Trident (Lt. A.J.W. Pitt, DSO, RN) conducted exercises in the Clyde area. These included RD/F exercises with HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) and HMS Shikari (Lt. E.H.U. Cautley, RNVR). (7)
7 Aug 1944 HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) conducted exercises in the Clyde area with HMS Shikari (Lt. E.H.U. Cautley, RNVR). (6)
9 Aug 1944 HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) conducted A/S exercises in the Clyde area with HMS Bulldog (Lt.Cdr. C.G. Walker, RN). (6)
11 Aug 1944 HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) conducted log trialsin Loch Long. (6)

12 Aug 1944 HMS Trident (Lt. A.J.W. Pitt, DSO, RN), HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR), HMS Varne (Lt. I.G. Raikes, DSC, RN) and HMS Bridgewater (Cdr.(Retd.) R.H. Mack, RN) conducted exercises in the Clyde area.
Upon completion of these exercises HMS Vitality, HMS Varne and HMS Bridgewater proceeded to Campbeltown while HMS Trident proceeded to Holy Loch.

During the night of 12 / 13 August 1944, HMS Varne, HMS Vitality and HMS Bridgewater conducted night exercises off Campbeltown. Upon completion of these exercises all ships proceeded to Holy Loch. (6)
13 Aug 1944 HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) shifted from Holy Loch to Rothesay. (6)

14 Aug 1944 Around 1800 B/14, HMS Upright (Lt. J.A.L. Wilkinson, RN), HMS United (Lt. M.D. Hutley, RNR) and HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) departed Rothesay under escort of HMS Milford (Lt.Cdr. G.G. Slade, RN).

The next day, around 1250 B/15, HMS United and HMS Upright parted company to cross the Atlantic and proceed to St.Johns, Newfoundland. They were escorted by HMS Northern Sky (T/Lt. G.O.T.D. Henderson, RNVR) which had just joined.
HMS Vitality and HMS Milford continued on northwards. (8)
15 Aug 1944 HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) arrived at Tobermory. (6)
16 Aug 1944 HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) conducted exercises off Tobermory with HMS Fowey (A/Lt.Cdr. G.E. Newey, RNR) and HMCS Loch Morlich (T/A/Lt.Cdr. L.L. Foxall, RCNR). (6)
17 Aug 1944 HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) conducted exercises off Tobermory with HMS Fowey (A/Lt.Cdr. G.E. Newey, RNR) and HMCS Petrolia (T/Lt. P.W. Spragge, RCNVR). (6)
18 Aug 1944 HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) conducted exercises off Tobermory with HMS Fowey (A/Lt.Cdr. G.E. Newey, RNR) and HMCS Petrolia (T/Lt. P.W. Spragge, RCNVR). (6)
19 Aug 1944 HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) conducted exercises off Tobermory with HMCS Petrolia (T/Lt. P.W. Spragge, RCNVR). (6)
22 Aug 1944 HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) conducted exercises off Tobermory with HMS Fowey (A/Lt.Cdr. G.E. Newey, RNR) and HMCS Loch Morlich (T/A/Lt.Cdr. L.L. Foxall, RCNR). (6)

26 Aug 1944 HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) conducted exercises off Tobermory with HMCS Loch Morlich (T/A/Lt.Cdr. L.L. Foxall, RCNR) and HMS Morpeth Castle (T/A/Lt.Cdr. E.R. Pate, DSC, RNR). (6)
27 Aug 1944 HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) conducted exercises off Tobermory with HMCS Ribble (T/A/Lt.Cdr. A.B. Taylor, RCNR) and HMCS Loch Achanalt (T/Lt. R.W. Hart, RCNVR). (6)

27 Sep 1944 HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) conducted exercises off Larne with HMS Philante (Cdr. M.J. Evans, OBE, RN). (9)

30 Sep 1944 HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) conducted exercises off Larne with HMS Bridgewater (Cdr.(Retd.) R.H. Mack, RN) and HMS Jan van Gelder (Lt. P.D. O'Driscoll, RNR). Following the exercises with these surface ships HMS Vitality conducted gunnery and night exercises. (9)
1 Oct 1944 HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) returned to Holy Loch. (10)
3 Oct 1944 HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) conducted noise trials in Loch Goil. (10)
8 Oct 1944 HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) conducted noise trials in Loch Goil. (10)

9 Oct 1944 During the night of 9 / 10 October 1944, HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR), conducted night exercises in the Clyde area. Upon completion of these exercises HMS Vitality proceeded to Larne. (9)
12 Oct 1944 HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) departed Holy Loch for her 1st war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in the North Sea on an anti-uboat patrol. This was a work-up patrol.

Passage north through the Minches was made together with HMS Selene (Lt.Cdr. H.R.B. Newton, DSC, RN) and HMS Sceptre (Lt. I.S. McIntosh, MBE, DSO, DSC, RN). They were escorted by HMS Sardonyx (A/Lt.Cdr. T.A. Easton, RNVR).
For the daily positions of HMS Vitality during this patrol see the map below.
29 Oct 1944 HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) ended her 1st war patrol at Lerwick. The patrol had been uneventful. (11)

31 Oct 1944 HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) departed Lerwick for Holy Loch. She made the passage together with HMS Selene (Lt.Cdr. H.R.B. Newton, DSC, RN) and HMS Sceptre (Lt. I.S. McIntosh, MBE, DSO, DSC, RN). They were escorted by HMS Sardonyx (A/Lt.Cdr. T.A. Easton, RNVR). (10)
2 Nov 1944 HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) arrived at Holy Loch.
Vitality was now assigned to training duties for which she joined the 7th Submarine Flotilla. (12)
11 Nov 1944 HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) was docked at Holy Loch. (12)
13 Nov 1944 HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) was undocked. (12)
15 Nov 1944 HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) shifted from Holy Loch to Campbeltown. (12)

18 Nov 1944 HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) conducted A/S exercises off Campbeltown with HMS La Cordeliere (Lt.Cdr. A.J.G. Barff, RNR). (12)

20 Nov 1944 HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) conducted A/S exercises off Campbeltown with HMS Shemara (Cdr.(Retd.) H. Buckle, RN) and HMS Altair (T/Lt. J.L. James, RNR). (12)
21 Nov 1944 HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) conducted A/S exercises off Campbeltown with HMS La Cordeliere (Lt.Cdr. A.J.G. Barff, RNR). (12)

28 Nov 1944 HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) conducted A/S exercises off Campbeltown with HMS Berkeley Castle (Cdr. N.W. Duck, DSC and Bar, RD, RNR), HMS Lancaster Castle (T/Lt. W.S. Joliffe, RNR) and HMS Dumbarton Castle (T/A/Lt.Cdr. R.C. Heron, RNVR). (12)

Dryden-Dymond, SANF(V)), HMS Alnwick Castle (A/Lt.Cdr. H.A. Stonehouse, DSC, RNR) and HMS Seabear (T/A/Lt.Cdr. A.R. Patton, RNVR). (13)

21 Dec 1944 HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with HMS Snowdrop (Lt. J.B. Hogg, RNR), HMS Alnwick Castle (A/Lt.Cdr. H.A. Stonehouse, DSC, RNR) and HMSAS Good Hope (T/Lt.Cdr. R.P. Dryden-Dymond, SANF(V)). (13)

27 Dec 1944 HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with HMS Eday (T/Lt. G.G. Chisholm, RNVR), HMS Imperialist (T/Lt. L.G.H. Mole, RNVR), HMS Loch Craggie (T/A/Lt.Cdr. C.L.L. Davies, RNVR) and HMS St. Helena (T/A/Lt.Cdr. W.G. Pardoe-Matthews, RNR). (13)

28 Dec 1944 HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with HMS St. Helena (T/A/Lt.Cdr. W.G. Pardoe-Matthews, RNR), HMS Seabear (T/A/Lt.Cdr. A.R. Patton, RNVR), HMS Snowdrop (Lt. J.B. Hogg, RNR) and HMS Eday (T/Lt. G.G. Chisholm, RNVR). (13)
29 Dec 1944 HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with HMS Oakham Castle (T/A/Lt.Cdr. A.H. Lyons, RNVR) and HMS Snowdrop (Lt. J.B. Hogg, RNR). (13)
30 Dec 1944 HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) conducted gunnery exercises off Tobermory.
[No log is available for HMS Vitality for January 1945, therefore no details of her operations during that month can be given.] (13)
10 Jan 1945 HMS Upshot (Lt. A.J. Boyall, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with aircraft. Also attack exercises were carried out together with HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR). (14)
18 Jan 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) arrived at Rothesay from Tobermory. (15)
5 Feb 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) conducted exercises in the Clyde area with HMS Safari (Lt.Cdr.(Retd.) J.R.G. Harvey, RN). (16)
6 Feb 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) conducted exercises in the Clyde area. (16)
8 Feb 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) conducted gunnery exercises in the Clyde area. (16)
10 Feb 1945 HMS H 50 (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) conducted exercises in the Clyde area with HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR). (17)
11 Feb 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) shifted from Rothesay to Campbeltown. (16)
12 Feb 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) conducted exercises off Campbeltown. (16)
14 Feb 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown. After about two hours the exercises were cancelled. (16)
15 Feb 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown. (16)
16 Feb 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown. (16)
18 Feb 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown. (16)
20 Feb 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown. (16)
21 Feb 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown. After about one hour the exercises were cancelled. (16)
22 Feb 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown. (16)
23 Feb 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown. (16)
25 Feb 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown. (16)
26 Feb 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown. (16)
27 Feb 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown. (16)
28 Feb 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown. (16)
1 Mar 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown. (2)
2 Mar 1945 Around 0200 A/2, HMS Uproar (Lt. J.N. Devlin, DSC, RN) departed Rothesay for Tobermory. She was escorted by HMS Milford (Cdr.(Retd.) G.G. Slade, RN).
At 0500 A/2, they were joined by HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) which came from Campbeltown.
They all arrived at Tobermory shortly before 1700 A/2. (2)
3 Mar 1945 HMS Uproar (Lt. J.N. Devlin, DSC, RN) and HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) proceeded from Tobermory for Loch Alsh. They were escorted by HMS Milford (Cdr.(Retd.) G.G. Slade, RN). (18)
4 Mar 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) participated in A/S exercises off Tobermory. (2)
5 Mar 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) participated in A/S exercises off Tobermory. (2)
6 Mar 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) participated in A/S exercises off Tobermory. (2)
8 Mar 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) participated in A/S exercises off Tobermory. (2)
9 Mar 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) participated in A/S exercises off Tobermory. (2)
12 Mar 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) participated in A/S exercises off Tobermory. (2)

14 Mar 1945 During 14/15 March 1945, HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) and HMS Uproar (Lt. J.N. Devlin, DSC, RN) conducted exercises in the area to the north of Rona Island in the Minches. These included night exercises.
They entered Portree Bay on the 15th and returned to Loch Alsh on the 16th. (18)
22 Mar 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) and HMS Uproar (Lt. J.N. Devlin, DSC, RN) conducted exercises off Loch Alsh. (18)
23 Mar 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) participated in A/S exercises off Tobermory. (2)
24 Mar 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) conducted exercises off Tobermory with HMS Philante (Cdr. M.J. Evans, OBE, RN). (2)
25 Mar 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) conducted exercises off Tobermory. (2)
29 Mar 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with E.G. 30. (2)
31 Mar 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with E.G. 30. (2)
2 Apr 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) and HMS Uproar (Lt. J.N. Devlin, DSC, RN) conducted exercises off Loch Alsh. (19)
3 Apr 1945 HrMs O 23 (Lt.Cdr. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) conducted attack exercises off Loch Alsh during which HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) served as the target. (20)

4 Apr 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Loch Alsh with ships of the 2nd Escort Group which was made up of the sloop HMS Wild Goose (Cdr. D.E.G. Wemyss, DSO, DSC and Bar, RN) and the frigates HMS Loch Fada (Cdr. B.A. Rogers, RD, RNR), HMS Loch Ruthven (A/Lt.Cdr. R.T. Horan, RNR), HMS Dominica (Lt.Cdr. H.N. Lawson, RD, RNR), HMS Labuan (T/A/Lt.Cdr. V.D.H. Bidwell, DSC, RNR) and HMS Tobago (T/A/Lt.Cdr. J.H.L. May, OBE, RNVR). (21)

5 Apr 1945 HrMs O 23 (Lt.Cdr. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN), HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) and HMS Scotsman (Lt. A.H.B. Anderson, DSC, RNR) conducted A/S exercises off Loch Alsh with HMS Philante (Cdr. M.J. Evans, OBE, RN) and ships from the 2nd Escort Group which was made up of the sloop HMS Wild Goose (Cdr. D.E.G. Wemyss, DSO, DSC and Bar, RN) and the frigates HMS Loch Fada (Cdr. B.A. Rogers, RD, RNR), HMS Loch Ruthven (A/Lt.Cdr. R.T. Horan, RNR), HMS Dominica (Lt.Cdr. H.N. Lawson, RD, RNR), HMS Labuan (T/A/Lt.Cdr. V.D.H. Bidwell, DSC, RNR) and HMS Tobago (T/A/Lt.Cdr. J.H.L. May, OBE, RNVR). Also practice attacks were made on HMS Philante. (20)

6 Apr 1945 HMS Uproar (Lt. J.N. Devlin, DSC, RN) and HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Loch Alsh ship of the 2nd Escort Group which was made up of the sloop HMS Wild Goose (Cdr. D.E.G. Wemyss, DSO, DSC and Bar, RN) and the frigates HMS Loch Fada (Cdr. B.A. Rogers, RD, RNR), HMS Loch Ruthven (A/Lt.Cdr. R.T. Horan, RNR), HMS Dominica (Lt.Cdr. H.N. Lawson, RD, RNR), HMS Labuan (T/A/Lt.Cdr. V.D.H. Bidwell, DSC, RNR) and HMS Tobago (T/A/Lt.Cdr. J.H.L. May, OBE, RNVR). (19)

8 Apr 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Loch Alsh with ships of the 8th Escort Group which was made up of the frigates HMS Loch Achray (T/A/Lt.Cdr. L.H. Stammers, RNVR, with the Commanding Officer of the group, A/Cdr. J.H. Eaden, DSC and two Bars, RN, on board), HMS Loch Glendhu (Lt.Cdr. E.G.P.B. Knapton, RN), HMS Loch More (Lt.Cdr. R.A.D. Cambridge, DSC, RNR) and HMSAS Natal (T/Lt.Cdr. D.A. Hall, DSC, SANF(V)) (a fifth frigate of the Escort Group was undergoing repairs at Belfast and was not present). (21)

11 Apr 1945 HMS Uproar (Lt. J.N. Devlin, DSC, RN) and HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with ships of the 15th Escort Group which was at that moment made up of the frigates HMS Dacres (T/A/Lt.Cdr. R. Leigh-Wood, RNVR), HMS Inglis (T/A/Lt.Cdr. A.P. Cobbold, RNVR), HMS Loring (Lt. J.A. Ogilvy, RN) and HMS Mounsey (T/A/Lt.Cdr. G.C. Julian, RNZNVR). (19)

14 Apr 1945 At 1000 B/14, HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN), HMS Uproar (Lt. J.N. Devlin, DSC, RN) and HMS Satyr (Lt. T.S. Weston, DSO, DSC, RN) departed Loch Alsh for Oban where they arrived around 1930 B/14. They were escorted by HMS Kalan (T/Lt. H.E. Wagner, RNVR). (21)

15 Apr 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN), HMS Uproar (Lt. J.N. Devlin, DSC, RN) and HMS Satyr (Lt. T.S. Weston, DSO, DSC, RN) shifted from Oban to Rothesay. They were escorted by HMS Kihna (Cdr.(Retd.) T.J.T.C. Jenks, RN). (21)
27 Apr 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) conducted exercises in the Clyde area with HMS Hastings (A/Cdr. E.A. Stocker, DSC, RN). (21)
30 Apr 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) shifted from Rothesay to Campbeltown. (21)
1 May 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown. (22)
1 May 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) participated in exercises off Campbeltown. (22)
2 May 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) participated in exercises off Campbeltown. (22)
4 May 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) participated in exercises off Campbeltown. (22)
5 May 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) shifted from Campbeltown to Holy Loch were she was immediately docked. (22)
7 May 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) was undocked. (22)
8 May 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) shifted from Holy Loch to Rothesay. (22)
11 May 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) conducted exercises in the Clyde area. (22)
12 May 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) shifted from Rothesay to Campbeltown. (22)
15 May 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown. (22)
16 May 1945 HMS Vitality, (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Campbeltown with HMS Radiant (T/A/Lt.Cdr. A. Wallace, RNVR) and HMS Bedlington (A/Ch.Skr. A.W. Peak, RNR). (22)

18 May 1945 HMS Vitality, (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Campbeltown with HMS Ambuscade (A/Lt.Cdr. H.G. Chesterman, DSC and Bar, RNR) and HMS Borage (T/Lt. E.A. Hitchcock, RNR). (22)
20 May 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown. (22)
21 May 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown. (22)
22 May 1945 HMS Vitality, (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Campbeltown with HMS Shemara (Cdr.(Retd.) H. Buckle, RN). (22)
23 May 1945 HMS Vitality, (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Campbeltown with HMS St. Modwen (T/A/Lt.Cdr. F.J.G. Rice, RNR) and HMS ML 472 (T/Lt. J.M.W. Hardie, RNVR). (22)
24 May 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) conducted exercises off Campbeltown with HMS Astravel (T/Lt. D.E. Lewis, RNVR). (22)
25 May 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown. (22)
27 May 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown. (22)
30 May 1945 HMS Vitality, (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Campbeltown with HMS Shemara (Cdr.(Retd.) H. Buckle, RN) and HMS St. Modwen (T/A/Lt.Cdr. F.J.G. Rice, RNR). (22)
31 May 1945 HMS Vitality, (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Campbeltown with HMS Altair (T/Lt. J.L. James, RNR) and HMS Bedlington (A/Ch.Skr. A.W. Peak, RNR). (22)
1 Jun 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown. (23)
2 Jun 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) conducted special trials off Campbeltown with HMS Astravel (T/Lt. D.E. Lewis, RNVR). (23)
3 Jun 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) participated in exercises off Campbeltown. (23)
4 Jun 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown. (23)
6 Jun 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown. (23)

10 Jun 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) participated in exercises off Campbeltown. (23)
11 Jun 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown. (23)
13 Jun 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) conducted special trials off Campbeltown with HMS Unsparing (T/Lt. J.D. Tweedie, RNVR) and HMS Astravel (T/Lt. D.E. Lewis, RNVR). (23)
14 Jun 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown. (23)

18 Jun 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) shifted from Campbeltown to Rothesay. (23)
22 Jun 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) shifted from Rothesay to Holy Loch where she was immediately docked. (23)
23 Jun 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) was undocked at Holy Loch. She immediately proceeded to Rothesay. (23)
29 Jul 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) departed Rothesay for Portsmouth. (24)
1 Aug 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) arrived at Portsmouth. (25)
7 Aug 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) was placed in reserve. (25)
29 Aug 1945 HMS Uproar, HMS Unrivalled, HMS Vitality and HMS Urtica departed Portsmouth for Londonderry (Lough Foyle).
1 Sep 1945 HMS Uproar, HMS Unrivalled, HMS Vitality and HMS Urtica arrived at Londonderry (Lough Foyle) from Portsmouth.
ADM numbers indicate documents at the British National Archives at Kew, London.
